content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 29,2004
Julia Miller, Councilmember
City of Sunnyvale
Post Office Box3707
Sunnyvale, CA 9 4088-37 07
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-04-035
Dear Ms. Miller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since there is rro prorp""tive specific
decision or proceedlng on which we can advise, we are treating yo* r"qrr"rt as one for
informal assistance.' Our informal assistance is based on the iu.t. yoo provide. The
Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission') does not act as a finder of fact when
it provides informal assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Nothing in this
letter should be construed to advise on past conduct.
QUESTION
May you use campaign funds to pay for a lunch meeting with business delegation
representatives from China, should they request to meet with you and other local
officials?
CONCLUSION
You may use campaign funds to pay for this luncheon meeting if you can show
that the meal for you, the representatives from China and additional Sunnyvale local
officials is reasonably related to a political, legislative or govemmental purpose.
I Gorrernment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not p.o-ula" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation r8329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
Re:
s;


File No. I-04-035
Page No. 2
FACTS
A delegation of dignitaries and elected officials from Anqing and Anhui China
periodically visit the United States. During these visits, they may be in the San Francisco
area. You would like to extend an invitation to meet them over lunch, and would like to
know whether you could use campaign funds to pay for this meal.
In a recent conversation with Commission stafi, it was discussed that the original
visit described in your letter had already occurred on February 23,2o04. T'he
Commission cannot advise you on this past conduct. However, you indicated that a
similar visit would occur againin the future. You requested informal advice as to
whether you could use campaign funds for a future lunch meeting. You have stated that
in any future visit, any lunch meeting would include yourself, the members of the
Chinese delegation, and other current city officials from Sunnyvale may also be in
attendance. You have also indicated that the pulpose of the visit would be to discuss
currerrt and potential areas of cooperation for business relationships among the cities.
ANALYSIS
1. Expenditure of Funds for Third Parties
The Political Reform Act's provisions concerning the permissible uses of
campaign funds are found in sections 89510-89522. The general rule of the personal use
law is that absent an express provision pertaining to the expendifure, an expenditure of
campaigr^r funds must be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose.3
You have indicated that you would like to expend campaign funds to cover the
costs of a lunch for visiting dignitaries from China, and for other local officials. The
purpose of this lunch is to discuss current and potential areas of cooperation for business
relationships amongst Sunnyvale, other local cities, and cities in China. Therefore, you
may use campaign funds to purchase lunch for the visiting officials, as well as otherlocal
govefirmental officials, because the meal would be reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or govemmental purpose.
2. Expenditure of Funds which Directly Benefit the Official
Certain specified uses of campaign funds are considered to confer a substantial
personal benefit on the candidate or elected official, and must meet a higher political,
legislative, or govemmental standard. ($ 89512; $ 89512.5.) "substantial personal
benefit" is defined to mean "an expenditure of campaign funds which r"srrits in a direct
personal benefit with a value of more than two hundred dollars ($ 2OOl to a candidate,
elected officer, or any individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure
of campaign funds held by a committee." ($ 89511(bX3).) Moreover, regulation 1g960
provides that an expenditure of campaign funds results in a "direct personal benefit"
3 Please note, if funds used are surplus campaign funds, additional restrictions may apply.


File No. I-04-035
Page No. 3
when a member of the candidate or elected official's immediate family realizes an
increase in his or her income of more than $200 from the expenditure. (Regulation
18e60(a)(1).)
In the Sloan Advice Letter (No. 4-93-023) the San Diego Police Officers
Association wanted to use campaign funds from its political action committee to host a
dinner for delegates and congressional attendees of the National Association of Police
Organizations'annual conference. The Commission determined that costs of the dinner
were directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose because the
purpose of the conference was to educate and inform delegates and congressional guests
on the national agenda for law enforcement issues, and to allow guests to meet and
convey the concems of the committee and its members.
In order to use campaign funds for you to attend the meal, you must meet the
higher standard of "directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose,,
because you are being conferred a substantial personal benefit by attanding the lunch
meeting. You have indicated that the purpose of this meeting is to discuss current and
potential areas of cooperation for business relationships amongst the three cities.
Therefore, use of campaign funds for this meal would be directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: el/,**Rrtr
Allison Rector
Intern, LegalDivision
ARje

2 Informal assistance does not p.o-ula" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation r8329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
Re:
s;
